rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set 1 & Demands For Documents, Set 1
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 12, Plaintiffs' (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories, Set 1 & Demands For Documents, Set 1. On its own motion the court intends to appoint a discovery referee and refer all these motions to the referee. The parties are to meet and confer in an effort to agree on a referee. If the parties agree on a referee, they must notify the court of the name of the agreed referee by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org before the hearing and thereafter submit a proposed order. In such event, the motion will be taken off calendar. Otherwise, hearing required. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY HOULIHAN VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 13, Defendant Central Striping Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Responses To Admission/Interrogatories. The court adopts the recommendation of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne as follows: Defendant Central Stripping Services' Motion to Compel Further Responses to Requests for Admission 5, 6, 7, 8 & 10 is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve no later than September 21, 2012 his response to Requests for Admission 5, 6, 7, 8, & 10. If a response to any such Request is anything other than an unqualified admission, Plaintiff will provide with specificity the information required by Form Interrogatory no. 17.1 as to such request. Plaintiff's initial responses to the form interrogatories were not timely, thus all objections are waived. Plaintiff and his counsel will pay to Defendant Central Stripping Services sanctions in the amount of $2,500.00. The court adds a due date of October 12, 2012 for payment. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. ADAM BURGESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Qusah Service Of Summons And Complaint; Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANTS ADAM BURGESS and LINDSAY BURGESS' Motion To Qusah Service Of Summons And Complaint. Denied. Regardless of whether Adam and Lindsay Burgess are domiciled in California, Plaintiffs satisfied their burden of showing general jurisdiction over Adam and Lindsay Burgess. Per defendants' notice of motion, only Adam and Lindsay Burgess filed this motion to quash. The hotel entity did not move to quash. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: STEVEN GOLDBERG VS. CANNON CONSTRUCTION INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF STEVEN GOLDBERG Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Parties to meet and confer, and advise Court as to anticipated length of all expert witness depositions currently scheduled. This motion will be taken under submission until a date and time to be set by the Court. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CASSAUNDRA ELLENA VS. STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. There is a misjoinder of parties in the Second Amended Complaint as DOI's approval of the insurance policy and Standard Insurance Company's denial of benefits do not involve the same transaction, occurrence or series of transactions or occurrences. Ms. Ellena has not shown that the misjoinder may be cured by amendment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CASSAUNDRA ELLENA VS. STANDARD INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT DAVID JONES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Ellena has not sufficiently alleged a violation of a specific mandatory duty. A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving Respondent's exercise of discretion. California Code of Regulations, title 10, section 2218.10(a) provides specific mandatory duties on the insurer, and not Respondent. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
